Now I will teach you silent.<[silence:60ms]>

Silent is like night when no beast growls, no wind howls, no fire cracks.<[silence:60ms]> It is when ear hears nothing—not footstep, not leaf rustle, not own breath.<[silence:60ms]> Like holding breath under water, but without water.<[silence:60ms]> If you cup hands over ears and hear only blood in head—that is close to silent.<[silence:60ms]> When all sound vanishes, what remains is silent.<[silence:60ms]>
clean_story:
Now I will teach you silent.

Silent is like night when no beast growls, no wind howls, no fire cracks. It is when ear hears nothing—not footstep, not leaf rustle, not own breath. Like holding breath under water, but without water. If you cup hands over ears and hear only blood in head—that is close to silent. When all sound vanishes, what remains is silent.
